questions.question: 1. Compare publish–subscribe and client–server models. Which statement is correct?

answers.a: Client–server allows many clients to send messages without a server
answers.b: Publish–subscribe requires direct connection between publisher and subscriber
answers.c: Publish–subscribe uses a broker to route messages between publishers and subscribers

questions.question: 2. Why is MQTT preferred in battery-powered IoT devices?

answers.a: It consumes more power than HTTP
answers.c: It is lightweight and uses low power
answers.d: It does not support sleep modes

questions.question: 3. How does QoS improve message delivery in MQTT?

answers.b: By controlling how reliably messages are delivered
They define channels where messages are published and subscribed
answers.c: They are used only for authentication

questions.question: 5. What happens when ESP8266 loses internet connection during MQTT publish?

answers.a: Message is always delivered later automatically
answers.b: Publish fails and may be retried depending on QoS
answers.c: Broker sends the data using SMS

questions.question: 6. Which is an example use case of MQTT in smart homes?

answers.b: Controlling lights and switches using publish–subscribe messages

questions.question: 7. What is the difference between MQTT broker and subscriber?

answers.a: Broker publishes sensor data; subscriber receives it
answers.c: Broker routes messages; subscriber receives messages from topics
answers.d: Subscriber connects devices to the internet

questions.question: 8. How can multiple sensors send data using a single MQTT topic?

answers.b: By sending data through different brokers
answers.c: By publishing structured or labeled data to the same topic
It uses very small packet sizes and minimal overhead

questions.question: 10. How can dashboards like Node-RED or MQTT Explorer visualize MQTT data?

answers.b: By connecting as subscribers and displaying incoming messages
answers.c: By converting MQTT into SMS alerts
answers.d: By sending data directly to Arduino pins
